commonIssues: Aggressive spreader: spreads by underground rhizomes and surface runners and can become invasive. Always grow in a contained pot or with a root barrier (Missouri Botanical Garden; UC ANR).
Mints grow better in wide-mouthed containers than deep ones; use a pot at least 15 cm (6 in) deep with a large surface area, or a ~19 L (5-gallon) container with added drainage holes (UC ANR).

Keep soil consistently moist; small containers dry out quickly in hot weather and may need water more than once a day. Spearmint tolerates wet soil but should not be left bone dry (UC ANR; Missouri Botanical Garden).
Deciduous: top growth dies back in winter and regrows from the crown/rhizomes in spring (RHS).

Frequent harvesting and pruning keep plants bushy and flavorful; flowers (lilac/pink/white) appear July-August and shoots can be sheared back after flowering (Old Farmer's Almanac; Missouri Botanical Garden).

source.citation: RHS - Mentha spicata (spearmint), plant details: full sun or partial shade; moist but well-drained soil; hardiness H7 (hardy below -20C); deciduous; ultimate height 0.5-1 m, spread 1-1.5 m.
RHS - Mentha spicata: position full sun or partial shade; deciduous oval bright-green leaves; water newly planted specimens until established and provide summer irrigation for container-grown plants.

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder - Mentha spicata 'Kentucky Colonel': USDA zones 5-9; full sun to part shade; water medium to wet, thrives in rich moist soils and tolerates wet conditions; 1-2 ft tall and wide; rhizomatous perennial; blooms July-August; no serious pests/diseases but an aggressive spreader.
Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder - Mentha spicata: hardiness zones 5-9; mature size 1-2 ft; growth habit rhizomatous, spreads to form ground cover; bloom time July-August.

UC ANR (UC Cooperative Extension, Fresno) - 'So you want to grow mint!': full sun to part shade; moderate water, water when soil becomes dry in hot weather and note small containers dry out faster; use containers at least 6 inches deep, five-gallon buckets work well with added drainage; avoid terra cotta; mint spreads rapidly without a physical barrier.

Old Farmer's Almanac - Mint: Planting, Growing, and Harvesting: grow in pots to prevent spreading; keep soil evenly moist; minimal care, benefits from frequent picking and pruning; spreads by horizontal runners and underground rhizomes.

Confidence note: feedingProfile intervals (28-day light feeding, balanced NPK, container-leach adjustment) are a conservative estimate. Authoritative extension/RHS sources for spearmint emphasize rich, moist soil and minimal care but do not specify a numeric fertilizer interval; mint is a low-feed herb and over-fertilizing reduces essential-oil/flavor concentration. The targetSunHours value (6) is interpolated from 'full sun to part shade' guidance plus the widely cited 5-6 hours for best color/flavor; minSunHours/maxSunHours and heatStressC (32C) are conservative estimates not given as explicit numbers by the cited sources.
